---
name: Copilot Instructions
description: This file contains instructions for GitHub Copilot to assist with code generation and suggestions.
---

## Copilot Instructions

- When using GitHub Copilot, ensure that the suggestions align with the project's coding standards and best practices. This includes adhering to naming conventions, code structure, and documentation requirements.
- Use always Vitest for testing, as it is the preferred testing framework for this project. Ensure that all test cases are written using Vitest's syntax and features to maintain consistency across the codebase.
- Test files should be written in a way that are esasy to understand and maintain. Use descriptive test case names and ensure that the tests cover a variety of scenarios, including edge cases.
- use given-when-then structure for test cases to improve readability and maintainability. This structure helps to clearly define the context, action, and expected outcome of each test case.
- use arrange-act-assert structure for test cases to improve readability and maintainability. This structure helps to clearly define the setup, execution, and verification steps of each test case.
